letter_quality.face_width_note: 15-dot glyphs for 15 pitch and condensed LQ are made from 29 dots by printer firmware.

regions.name: external_prom
regions.selection: write bit 7 low at F001h when correct external PROM is mounted

regions.name: 4M_CG
regions.size_note: manual label; likely 4 Mbit capacity class if populated, but confirm from chip markings

regions.name: 1M_CG
regions.size_note: manual label; likely 1 Mbit capacity class if populated, but confirm from chip markings
regions.location_note: text conflicts with PSRAM at 2C; Figure 2-8 appears to place 4C in the 8000h-A000h window for bank-selector values 40h..79h

board_photo_observations.observation: Online board photos appear to show a socketed E01A05LC at 6C, a socketed ROM-like device at 4C, a clearly marked RAM chip at 2C, and a partially obscured socket that is almost certainly 3C, with 5C unpopulated and labeled 4M/2M/1M/256kbit MASK IMPROM.
board_photo_observations.interpretation: 6C is likely the address-decoder gate array, not a ROM dump target. 3C is likely the program PROM, with M25A10PA plausibly indicating a 256 kbit / 32 KB mask PROM. 4C is the resident ROM/CG; a patched minipro custom PROM profile captured it as a stable 1 Mbit / 128 KiB image. The schematic reportedly identifies 4C pin 1 as A15, confirming the two 32 KiB halves inside each 64 KiB bank as real A15 address halves. It also shows J5 tying pin 1/A15 to BK2 on the 6C E01A05KA, J6 wiring pin 20 /CE, and the hard-to-read A16/OE label appears to be A16 wired to BK3. Figure 2-8 appears to place 4C in the CPU 8000h-A000h window for bank-selector values 40h..79h. Native T48 27C011-style reads mirrored the A16-low bank, but the custom bitbang profile produced a distinct pin-22-high/A16-high bank and a 128 KiB image ordered as A16-low then A16-high. 5C may be an alternate or optional mask-ROM footprint, not necessarily a populated stock CG ROM.
board_photo_observations.conflict: Actual PCB/chip markings still need to confirm the 3C and 4C socket roles.

download_buffer.note: DIP SW2-5 ON selects 8 KB input buffer; the technical manual says downloading will be ignored in that setting.

external_source_checks.source: IBMulator printer emulation / 1541 Ultimate MPS Emulator
external_source_checks.result: MPS Emulator includes Epson FX-80/JX-80, IBM Graphics Printer, IBM Proprinter, and Commodore MPS interpreters, but uses shared MPS chargen tables rather than a Proprinter III ROM dump.
external_source_checks.relevance_to_lq500: Not a direct LQ-500 source; at most useful as a 9-pin/FX-style reference.
